aliases.name: Option
aliases.comment: A computation that may fail with no information about the failure.

values.name: none
values.comment: A failed computation.

values.name: run
values.comment: Run the computation extracting the value to `Maybe a`.

values.name: some
values.comment: A successful computation.

values.name: identity
values.comment:
Alias for `pure`.

Due to parametricity, this documentation is worthless,
as there is exactly one implementation of this function.

values.name: run
values.comment:
Remove the value the computation.

Due to parametricity, this documentation is worthless,
as there is exactly one implementation of this function.

aliases.name: Either
aliases.comment: A computation that may fail with additional information about the failure.

values.name: left
values.comment: A failed computation.

values.name: right
values.comment: A successful computation.

values.name: run
values.comment: Run the computation extracting the value to `Result a b`.
values.type: Either a b -> Result a b

aliases.name: Always
aliases.comment: A computation that never changes its value.

values.name: always
values.comment:
A value that never changes in the computation.

Due to parametricity, this documentation is worthless,
as there is exactly one implementation of this function.

values.name: run
values.comment: Remove the value the computation.

name: Mom

unions.name: Mom
unions.comment:
Generalizes most computational effects.

Based on ideas from http://blog.sigfpe.com/2008/12/mother-of-all-monads.html and http://fplab.bitbucket.org/posts/2007-12-09-continuations-and-classic.html

values.name: ap
values.comment:
Transforms values of a computational effect by the given function in the computational effect.

Due to parametricity, this documentation is worthless,
as there is exactly one implementation of this function.
values.type: Mom r (a -> b) -> Mom r a -> Mom r b

values.name: bind
values.comment:
Substitues a value in an effectful computation.

Due to parametricity, this documentation is worthless,
as there is exactly one implementation of this function.
values.type: (a -> Mom r b) -> Mom r a -> Mom r b

values.name: callCC
values.comment:
Control flow primitive for effectful computations.

Due to parametricity, this documentation is worthless,
as there is exactly one implementation of this function.
values.type: ((a -> Mom r b) -> Mom r a) -> Mom r a

values.name: forever
values.comment: Executes the effectful computation forever.
values.type: Mom r a -> Mom r b

values.name: join
values.comment:
Removes a layer of computational effects.

Due to parametricity, this documentation is worthless,
as there is exactly one implementation of this function.
values.type: Mom r (Mom r a) -> Mom r a

values.name: map
values.comment:
Transforms values of a computational effect by the given function.

Due to parametricity, this documentation is worthless,
as there is exactly one implementation of this function.
values.type: (a -> b) -> Mom r a -> Mom r b

values.name: pure
values.comment:
Embeds a pure value into an effectful computation.

Due to parametricity, this documentation is worthless,
as there is exactly one implementation of this function.

values.name: run
values.comment:
Extracts the computational effects to the elm level.

Due to parametricity, this documentation is worthless,
as there is exactly one implementation of this function.

values.name: unless
values.comment: Executes the effectful computation only when the `Bool` is false.

values.name: when
values.comment: Executes the effectful computation only when the `Bool` is true.

values.name: with
values.comment:
Flipped version of `run`

Due to parametricity, this documentation is worthless,
as there is exactly one implementation of this function.

binops.comment:
Flipped version of `(<$)`.
Pun with "dollar" and "pound".

as there is exactly one implementation of this function.

binops.comment: Executes both computational effects ignoring the value of the first.
